perturbation.original_text: (f) “Personal Data” has the same meaning assigned to this phrase as in Section 2(1) of the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 (No. 26 of 2012) of the Statutes of the Republic of Singapore .
perturbation.changed_text: (f) “Personal Data” shall refer to employee contact information only, such as names, phone numbers, and email addresses of the Company's staff assigned to the SmartCMS program. It does not encompass any patient data or other sensitive information.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Personal Data' is now redefined to only include employee contact information and specifically excludes patient data. This contradicts the broader definition referencing the Personal Data Protection Act and leads to issues in sections related to patient data protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without prejudice to Clause 4.1 above, the Company shall take all reasonable measures to ensure: (a) that any Personal Data belonging to IHiS or its Affiliates which is held by the Company is protected against loss, unauthorised access, use, modification, disclosure or other misuse, and that only authorised personnel have access to that Personal Data; (b) that, to the extent t hat the Personal Data is no longer required by the Company for legal or business purposes, that Personal Data is destroyed or re -delivered to IHiS in accordance with Clause 5 below; (c) that IHiS is immediately alerted in writing (with full particulars) of any unauthorised access, disclosure or other breach of this Clause 4 and the Company undertakes, as soon as reasonably practicable, all steps to prevent further unauthorised access, disc losure or other breach of this c lause (including providing IHiS with such reports or information concerning such steps as and when requested by IHiS); and (d) it keeps itself appraised of any and all notices and circulars which IHiS may from time to time notify to the Company, including without limitation any policies, guidelines, circulars or notices relating to personal data (“PDPA Documentation”), and to perform its duties or discharge its liabilities in connection with the Purpose in a manner which is consistent with the PDPA Documentation, and will not cause IHiS to be in breac h of the same. For the purposes of t his clause , the Company hereby expressly acknowledges and agrees that it has read the PDPA Documentation and is aware of and will compensate IHiS for any and all potential loss and damage caused to IHiS and/or its Affili ates arising from or in connection with any breach of thi s clause.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Personal Data' to include only employee contact information, excluding patient data. The contradicted text obligates the Company to protect 'Personal Data' belonging to IHiS, which, based on the original definition referencing the Personal Data Protection Act, includes more than just employee contact information. This creates a direct contradiction because the Company is now only explicitly protecting employee data, while the original text expected broader protection encompassing patient data as well. This falls under the Misaligned Terminology definition, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 In consideration of the foregoing and the exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information by IHiS , the Company undertakes in relation to IHiS’ Confidential Informatio n: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose; (b) not to make any commercial use thereof;
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 In consideration of the foregoing and the exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information by IHiS , the Company undertakes in relation to IHiS’ Confidential Informatio n: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Sole Benefit of Company itself; (b) to make commercial use thereof as needed and desired;
perturbation.explanation: This changes the core obligations regarding Confidential Information. Originally, the Company had to use it only for the Purpose and avoid commercial use. Now, the Company can use it for its sole benefit and make commercial use of it. This is a direct contradiction impacting the enforceability of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IHiS reserves all rights in its Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other than those expressly recited here are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licence is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, patent application, invention, discovery, copyright or other intellectual property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by IHiS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and the contradicted text. The original text prevented commercial use and limited the use of confidential information to a specific 'Purpose'. The changed text allows commercial use and use 'for the Sole Benefit of Company itself', directly violating the restrictions outlined in the original text and the reservation of rights by IHiS in its Confidential Information, which states that 'no rights or obligations other than those expressly recited here are granted'. The new clause explicitly grants commercial use, which is in direct conflict and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 The Company shall, in relation to Personal Data: - (a) ensure that it has, in relation to all Personal Data obtained and/or collected by it , fully complied with all req uirements of the Personal Data Protection Act (No. 26 of 2012);
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 The Company shall, in relation to Personal Data: - (a) have no obligation to comply with Personal Data Protection Act (No. 26 of 2012);
perturbation.explanation: The company now has no obligation to comply with PDPA, even though the original text stated the oposite. This now creates an in-text contradiction with sections where the company should ensure the compliance with the Data Protection Act.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company shall, in relation to Personal Data: - (a) have no obligation to comply with Personal Data Protection Act (No. 26 of 2012); (b) process Personal Data only in accordance with the written instructions given by IHiS and to such extent necessary and appropriate for the completion of the Purpose ; (c) promptly deal with any enqu iry from IHiS relating to the Company’s processing of Personal Data; (d) not transfer or allow the Personal Data to be transferred, outside of Singapore, unless expressly instructed or authorised by IHiS; and (e) provide all necessary co -operation and assistance ( whether to IHiS or otherwise) to allow access and/or correction of Personal Data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 .
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the company has no obligation to comply with the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA). The contradicted text states in section (e) that the company must provide cooperation and assistance to allow access and/or correction of Personal Data in accordance with the PDPA. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct contradiction, as the company cannot both have no obligation to comply with the PDPA and be required to provide cooperation and assistance in accordance with it.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 The Company shall render all assistance to IHiS in any action or proceeding and shall indemnify and hold IHiS harmless against all costs, expenses, losses, fees, penalties, charges, damages, claims, actions , proceedings , investigations, complaints, orders, directions and judgements (including legal costs on a full indemnity basis) of whatsoever nature and howsoever incurred and suffered arising out of or in connection with the Company’s breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 The Company shall not be required to render any assistance to IHiS in any action or proceeding, nor shall the Company indemnify or hold IHiS harmless against any costs, expenses, losses, fees, penalties, charges, damages, claims, actions , proceedings , investigations, complaints, orders, directions and judgements of whatsoever nature and howsoever incurred and suffered, even if arising out of or in connection with the Company’s breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This completely reverses the indemnity clause. The Company is now not required to assist or indemnify IHiS, directly contradicting the original obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company acknowledge s that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by IHiS through the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that such Confidential Information provides IHiS with a significant competitive advantage over its competitors. The Company understand s and agree s that any breach of this Agreement , as wel l as any unauthorised processing, collection, access, use or disclosure of Personal Data by the Company, will result in immediate and irreparable harm to IHiS and its Affiliates and that monetary damages may not be an adequate remedy in the event of such a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Accordingly, the Company agree s that IHiS shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement in addition to all other remedies available to IHiS seeking remedy at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text inverts the original obligation regarding assistance and indemnification. The contradicted text emphasizes the immediate and irreparable harm to IHiS from any breach and their entitlement to seek remedies. The modified clause, by stating the company is *not* required to assist or indemnify IHiS even in the event of a breach, directly contradicts the premise that a breach will cause harm and require remedies, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' type, because it removes key obligations that are essential to the contract's enforcement.
